commit.message:
Add local workspace system for real build/test verification

Implement per-agent local git clones so agents can build, test, and
validate code before committing to GitHub. No more fabricated test
reports or unchecked code pushes.

New components:
- LocalWorkspace: per-agent git clone management (clone, sync, branch,
  commit, push, cleanup) via Process.Start
- BuildRunner: executes build commands with error parsing for dotnet,
  npm, and generic build systems
- TestRunner: runs tests with real output parsing (xUnit, NUnit, Jest,
  pytest) and FormatResultsAsMarkdown()
- WorkspaceConfig: configuration model (RootPath, build/test commands,
  timeouts, retry counts)

Integration changes:
- EngineerAgentBase: local build/test loop with AI fix-retry before
  committing. Falls back to API-only when workspace not configured.
- TestEngineerAgent: actually runs dotnet test and includes real
  results in PR body instead of fabricated reports
- PullRequestWorkflow: new CreatePrForPushedBranchAsync for push-based
  PR creation
- WorkspaceCleanupMessage: PE broadcasts cleanup signal when all
  engineering tasks complete
- All engineer subclasses updated to pass BuildRunner/TestRunner

Design principles:
- Opt-in via config (backward compatible, API-only is default)
- Each agent gets isolated workspace directory
- Build must pass before code reaches GitHub
- Test results in PRs are from actual execution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add InputCortex keystroke agent and SHM pipeline (#29)

* Add AI handoff pipeline for cross-session context transfer

Generates role-scoped payloads (researcher, builder, auditor, gatherer, full)
within 5K word budgets for AI-to-AI session handoffs. Includes dispatch
envelope generation for task delegation between AI sessions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Add InputCortex keystroke intelligence agent + SHM pipeline

InputCortex: Phone keystroke processing agent with daemon mode (HTTP
listener + file watcher). Classifies input by app/content, scrubs
PHI/PII, detects credential entry, extracts intent signals, and
stores encrypted context blocks for behavioral analysis.

SHM Pipeline: Conference PDF processor + online collector daemon.
Extracts citations, statistics, clinical tools, and evidence levels
from PDFs. Daemon mode searches PubMed + SHM for hospital medicine
content on a rotating schedule.

Both expose skill manifests for AI dispatch integration so Guardian
One agents and external AI sessions can invoke them programmatically.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Address PR review feedback on InputCortex + SHM pipeline

P1 bug fixes:
- shm_pipeline.py: wrap finditer() in list() before slicing (TypeError)
- main.py --cortex-daemon: periodically call run() to flush stale sessions
  (previously the daemon only slept and never triggered persistence)

Security:
- InputCortex HTTP listener now binds to 127.0.0.1 by default (was 0.0.0.0)
- All endpoints now require X-Cortex-Token header (token generated on init
  or supplied via CORTEX_AUTH_TOKEN env / listener_auth_token config)
- Added --cortex-bind CLI flag for explicit override

Correctness:
- InputStreamProcessor: _next_block_id now lock-protected (race fix)
- InputStreamProcessor: actually implements session_timeout_seconds via
  new flush_stale_sessions() using monotonic last-update tracking
- InputCortex._flush_stale_sessions now calls the real stale path instead
  of flushing ALL sessions every run cycle
- InputCortex.initialize() is now idempotent — does not reset processor
  state on repeated calls
- Implemented session_summary() method to match skill manifest advertisement

API hygiene:
- Added public drop_dir, data_dir, auth_token properties on InputCortex
- main.py uses public cortex.drop_dir (was accessing private _drop_dir)
- Removed redundant cortex.initialize() call (already done in register_agent)

Config / docs:
- guardian_config.yaml input_cortex block now annotates which settings are
  consumed vs forward-looking; added listener_bind
- main.py wires listener_port/listener_bind/daemon_mode from config.custom
- Clarified input_stream.py docstring: session JSON is plaintext at
  filesystem layer; Vault-backed encryption is roadmap, not current
- Skill manifest: encryption_at_rest now honest about current state
- guardian_handoff_pipe.py: section_current_state now loads live stats
  from shm_pipeline_output/pipeline_stats.json (was hardcoded)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Address Copilot review: bug fixes, hardening, and InputCortex tests

Security / operational hardening:
- main.py: auth token no longer printed to stdout by default. Added
  --cortex-show-token flag; default prints a hint telling the user how
  to retrieve/rotate the token (prevents leakage to shell history, tmux
  logs, systemd journal, CI output).
- input_cortex.py: drop-file archival now uses shutil.move() with a
  unique timestamp suffix instead of Path.rename(). Fixes Windows
  overwrite failures and cross-filesystem moves.

Correctness:
- shm_pipeline.py: output filenames now include parent-dir slug + content
  hash to avoid collisions when identically named PDFs are scanned from
  different directories.
- shm_pipeline.py: PDFExtract now stores tool_mentions (dict[str,int]).
  PipelineStats.top_tools / total_tool_mentions now reflect true mention
  frequency instead of per-PDF presence counts.
- shm_pipeline.py: _extract_text_from_pdf now catches runtime errors
  per extractor (corrupt / encrypted PDFs) and falls through to the
  next library instead of aborting the directory run.
- guardian_handoff_pipe.py: dispatch_id now uses sha256(task + ISO
  timestamp + 4-byte nonce) truncated to 12 hex chars. Python's builtin
  hash() is process-salted so it was neither stable nor collision-safe.
- guardian_handoff_pipe.py: json_count now excludes pipeline_stats.json
  (and any other metadata JSON) so the handoff payload accurately counts
  only extract files.
- guardian_handoff_pipe.py: vault text now references the real source
  (guardian_dependency_tracker.csv) instead of vault.py.

Tests (new):
- tests/test_input_cortex.py (28 tests) covers:
  * credential detection (field hint / input type / keyword signal) +
    that credentials never land in the redaction log
  * PHI/PII scrubbing for SSN, DOB, phone, email, card — with the raw
    values confirmed absent from processed_text
  * classification (Gmail->message, Chrome->search)
  * intent signal extraction (scheduling, meeting, urgency)
  * session flushing writes session JSON + index entries and clears
    in-memory state
  * flush_stale_sessions honors the configured timeout (does not
    flush active sessions)
  * query_index filters by category, app, and respects limit
  * concurrent _next_block_id calls produce no duplicate IDs
  * InputCortex.initialize() is idempotent (counters/token preserved)
  * session_summary works for both open and flushed sessions
  * manifest advertises loopback bind + X-Cortex-Token auth

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Update guardian_one/integrations/input_stream.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address round 2 Copilot review: security hardening + metrics accuracy

Security:
- input_stream.py: sanitize session_id in flush filenames to prevent
  path traversal (session_id comes from HTTP payloads and could contain
  "../" or path separators). Now only [A-Za-z0-9_-] are allowed.
- input_cortex.py: HTTP listener enforces a 2 MB max payload size
  (configurable via max_payload_bytes). Requests exceeding the limit
  get 413; missing Content-Length gets 411. Prevents memory-based DoS.

Correctness:
- input_stream.py: process_batch() now returns (block, BatchResult) tuple
  so callers can distinguish CREDENTIAL_REDACTED from TOO_SHORT.
  InputCortex.ingest_payload logs "batch_skipped" for benign skips
  instead of inflating _batches_redacted metrics.
- input_cortex.py: docstring updated to reflect actual storage model
  (local JSON, vault-backed encryption is roadmap, not current).
- shm_pipeline.py: skill_manifest trimmed to only list actually
  implemented skills. Removed get_extracts and export_payload (not
  callable); added export_all (maps to export_pipeline_data). Removed
  data_sources "guidelines" and "cms" (not implemented).

Tests:
- Added HTTP auth integration test: boots listener on ephemeral port,
  verifies 401 for missing/wrong token, 200 for correct token on both
  GET /status and POST /input.
- Updated all process_batch test call sites to unpack (block, result)
  tuple and assert correct BatchResult values.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Fix dispatch_id comment, session_id normalization, remove dead import

- guardian_handoff_pipe.py: comment now says "Unique ID" not
  "Deterministic ID" since seed includes randomness.
- input_stream.py: session_id normalized to "default" once before
  both the ContextBlock and session index use it, fixing inconsistent
  lookups when batch.session_id is empty.
- main.py: remove unused Teleprompter import (was never registered
  in _build_agents; import came from main merge).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Round 3: docstring alignment, filename safety, ephemeral port, sentinel cleanup

- shm_pipeline.py: module docstring skill list now matches the actual
  manifest (collect_online, export_all, not get_extracts/export_payload).
  search_shm() now calls raise_for_status() before parsing response.
- input_stream.py: session filenames use microsecond timestamps +
  12-char sha256 of full session_id to prevent collisions on concurrent
  flush_all()/flush_stale_sessions() calls.
- tests/test_input_cortex.py: HTTP auth test now probes for a free port
  via socket bind(0) instead of hardcoding 19473. Prevents CI flake.
- main.py: removed orphaned --sentinel* argparse flags (handler was
  never wired; sentinel module still available for standalone use).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Round 4: error response hardening, lint cleanup, test robustness

- input_cortex.py: HTTP error responses now return structured JSON
  ({"error": "bad_request"}) instead of str(e) to avoid leaking
  internal details. /query?limit= validated with 400 on malformed
  values. Removed unused signal import; deduplicated os import.
- shm_pipeline.py: fitz.open() now uses try/finally to prevent file
  descriptor leak if page iteration raises.
- tests/test_input_cortex.py: removed unused tempfile import. HTTP
  auth test now polls for readiness instead of fixed sleep(0.5).
- main.py: sentinel removal comment clarified.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Round 5: regex fix, concurrency safety, input validation, socket cleanup

Bug fix:
- input_stream.py: email PHI regex used [A-Z|a-z] which matched pipe
  character in TLDs. Fixed to [A-Za-z].

Concurrency:
- input_cortex.py: _process_drop_dir now guarded by _drop_lock so
  concurrent calls from watcher thread and periodic run() don't
  double-process the same files.
- shm_pipeline.py: SIGINT handler only installed when running on
  main thread (prevents ValueError in embedded/supervised scenarios).

Robustness:
- input_cortex.py: stop_daemon() now calls server_close() after
  shutdown() to release the listening socket immediately.
- input_cortex.py: start_daemon() validates mode against known values
  and raises ValueError on invalid input (prevents silent no-op daemon).
- input_cortex.py: /query limit clamped to 1..500 (prevents 0/negative).
- input_stream.py: query_index catches JSONDecodeError per line so a
  single corrupt JSONL entry doesn't break all queries.
- input_cortex.py: HTTP error responses already use structured JSON
  (no str(e) leakage) — confirmed in this round.

Pre-existing (not this PR):
- dev_coach CLI flags + config: orphaned from merge, same pattern as
  sentinel/teleprompter. Not modified in this PR.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Fix listener bind failure surfacing + session_summary JSONL resilience

- input_cortex.py: HTTPServer bind now wrapped in try/except OSError.
  On failure (port in use), logs listener_bind_failed at ERROR severity
  and sets _stop_event so the daemon loop exits instead of hanging
  silently with no listener running.
- input_cortex.py: session_summary() now catches JSONDecodeError per
  JSONL line (same pattern as query_index) so one corrupt entry doesn't
  break session lookups.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Fix diagram/docstring accuracy, quarantine failed drop files

- input_cortex.py: architecture diagram no longer labels session files
  as "(vault)" since vault encryption is roadmap only.
- input_cortex.py: auth_token docstring updated to reflect that auth is
  always enabled (token generated on init if not configured).
- input_cortex.py: failed drop files now moved to incoming/failed/ with
  a timestamp suffix instead of staying in place and causing infinite
  retry loops + log spam in the watcher.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Fix DevCoach NameError + daily_digest date range overflow

- main.py: add guarded `from guardian_one.agents.dev_coach import
  DevCoach` at the --dev-coach* usage site so isinstance() doesn't
  raise NameError (the top-level import was removed with Teleprompter).
- input_cortex.py: daily_digest() now filters entries to the requested
  calendar day only. Previously `since=date` returned all entries from
  that date onward, inflating stats when later days had data.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Round 7: JSONL/JSON resilience, TOCTOU fix, shutdown flush, NCBI params

Resilience:
- input_cortex.py: session_summary() catches JSONDecodeError/IOError on
  corrupt session JSON files (returns flushed_file_corrupt status).
- shm_pipeline.py: load_stats() catches corrupt pipeline_stats.json
  and falls back to empty stats instead of crashing CLI paths.
- shm_pipeline.py: get_all_results() skips corrupt JSONL lines instead
  of aborting export.

Concurrency:
- input_stream.py: flush_stale_sessions() now pops stale sessions under
  lock before writing, fixing TOCTOU where a concurrent thread could
  update a session between the staleness check and the flush. Extracted
  _write_session() shared by both flush_session and flush_stale_sessions.

Operational:
- main.py: daemon Ctrl+C now calls cortex.shutdown() after stop_daemon()
  so open sessions are flushed to disk instead of lost.
- shm_pipeline.py: PubMed requests now include NCBI tool/email params
  (configurable via NCBI_TOOL/NCBI_EMAIL env vars) per E-utilities
  guidelines to reduce throttling risk.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* SHM pipeline: dedup results, persist --search, uncap export

- collect_cycle() now deduplicates against existing result_ids in
  online_results.jsonl before appending, preventing inflated counts
  when the same PubMed article matches multiple topics or cycles.
- --search now persists results to online_results.jsonl (with dedup)
  so stats.total_online_results and --export stay consistent.
- export_pipeline_data() passes limit=0 to get_all_results() for
  unlimited export instead of silently truncating at 200.
- get_all_results() now treats limit=0 as unlimited.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Re-insert stale sessions on write failure to prevent data loss

flush_stale_sessions() now catches OSError during _write_session()
and re-inserts the session into memory with a fresh timestamp so
transient I/O failures (disk full, permissions) don't permanently
drop captured blocks.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Re-insert session on flush_session write failure

Same pattern as flush_stale_sessions: flush_session() now catches
OSError from _write_session() and re-inserts the session into memory
so transient I/O failures don't permanently lose collected blocks.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Round 8: ThreadingHTTPServer, drop-file privacy, digest fixes, lint

Security / privacy:
- input_cortex.py: HTTP listener now uses ThreadingHTTPServer so a
  slow/stalled client can't DoS the single-threaded request handler.
- input_cortex.py: drop-file archival now deletes the raw payload
  (which may contain plaintext text) and writes a metadata-only
  receipt. This aligns with the privacy guarantee that raw typed
  content is never stored on disk.

Correctness:
- input_cortex.py: daily_digest() removed unused next_day variable;
  filter now uses == instead of <= for exact calendar day match.
- input_cortex.py: app_usage in daily_digest splits words evenly
  across apps in multi-app sessions (was double-counting).
- input_cortex.py: run() digest comment fixed (was "if past midnight"
  but no midnight gating exists).

Cleanup:
- guardian_handoff_pipe.py: added Windows cp* console encoding guard
  (same as main.py) to prevent UnicodeEncodeError on legacy terminals.
- shm_pipeline.py: removed unused _result_counter from OnlineCollector.
- tests/test_input_cortex.py: removed unused threading import.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Trim SHM daemon docstring to implemented sources only

Removed claims about clinical guidelines, QI registries, and CMS
policy sources from the module docstring. Only PubMed and SHM.org
are currently implemented.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

* Merge-on-reinsertion for flush errors + reject negative Content-Length

- input_stream.py: flush_session/flush_stale_sessions now merge stale
  blocks into any newer session that arrived during the write attempt,
  instead of blindly overwriting. Prevents data loss when a new batch
  races with a failed flush.
- input_cortex.py: _read_body rejects negative Content-Length (< 0)
  alongside oversized payloads. Prevents read(-1) which blocks until
  EOF and can pin a handler thread indefinitely.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WhbjBeoqNAgC5onpooMZmu

fix: sanitize HTML before script extraction in appendHtmlWithScripts (#1507)


* fix: sanitize HTML before script extraction in appendHtmlWithScripts

Use DOMPurify.sanitize() with ADD_TAGS:['script'] and WHOLE_DOCUMENT
on the raw HTML before extracting scripts for re-execution. This runs
DOMPurify's mXSS protection (serialize + re-parse) on the source from
which scripts are extracted, closing DOM-confusion attack vectors that
could create phantom script elements via parser inconsistencies.

Previously, scripts were extracted from the raw unsanitized HTML via
DOMParser, bypassing DOMPurify entirely for the script extraction path.

Fixes #294

[tacacs] Fix race condition in change_and_wait_aaa_config_update (#23729)

### Description of PR
Fix an intermittent failure in `tacacs/test_accounting.py` caused by a
race condition in `change_and_wait_aaa_config_update`.

Fixes ADO #37409631
(https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_workitems/edit/37409631)

### Type of change
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Testbed and Framework(new/improvement)
- [ ] New Test case
    - [ ] Skipped for non-supported platforms
- [ ] Test case improvement

### Back port request
- [ ] 202205
- [ ] 202305
- [ ] 202311
- [ ] 202405
- [ ] 202411
- [ ] 202505
- [x] 202511

### Approach
#### What is the motivation for this PR?

`tacacs/test_accounting.py` intermittently fails with
`check_local_log_exist()` exhausting all 6 retries (~40 min total)
before finding zero accounting entries in syslog.

Root cause: `get_auditd_config_reload_timestamp()` greps journalctl for
`"audisp-tacplus re-initializing configuration"` — this line appears
when audisp-tacplus **starts** re-initialization, not when it
**finishes**. `wait_until()` returns as soon as the new timestamp is
detected, but audisp-tacplus is still mid-reconnect to auditd for
another ~1.4 seconds.

The test immediately truncates syslog and runs the accounting test
command during this gap. Events generated during re-init are never
captured by audisp-tacplus.

Timeline evidence (from elastictest plan 69d07000eb4653619e057afa):
```
13:41:59  audisp-tacplus[693]: "re-initializing configuration" (START)
13:41:59  wait_until detects new timestamp → returns immediately  ← TOO EARLY
13:42:00  cleanup_tacacs_log truncates /var/log/syslog
13:42:00  test SSH command runs                                    ← audisp-tacplus STILL re-initializing!
13:42:01.432  hostcfgd: "AAA Update: key=accounting, data=..." (FINISH)
```

#### How did you do it?

Add `time.sleep(3)` after `wait_until()` returns in
`change_and_wait_aaa_config_update` to allow audisp-tacplus to complete
reconnection to auditd before the test proceeds. The `time` module is
already imported.

#### How did you verify/test it?

Analyzed elastictest log from plan `69d07000eb4653619e057afa` —
identified the 1.4-second gap between audisp-tacplus re-init start and
hostcfgd AAA config write finish. A 3-second sleep covers this gap with
margin.

The issue is more pronounced after many prior audisp-tacplus reloads
(from preceding tests in the same run), which slow down the re-init
cycle and widen the race window. Reruns pass because the testbed is in a
fresh state.

#### Any platform specific information?

Affects all platforms running TACACS accounting tests.

#### Supported testbed topology if it's a new test case?

N/A — bug fix only.

**Affected test cases:**
- `test_accounting_local_only`
- `test_accounting_tacacs_and_local`
- `test_accounting_tacacs_and_local_all_tacacs_server_down`

### Documentation
N/A

commit.message:
[bgp] Replace pidof with pgrep -x to avoid syslog false-positive ERR (#23724)

### Description of PR

Summary: Replace `pidof orchagent` with `pgrep -x orchagent` in BGP
suppress-fib helpers to eliminate a syslog false-positive ERR.

Fixes # (issue)
37409594

### Type of change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Testbed and Framework(new/improvement)
- [ ] New Test case
    - [ ] Skipped for non-supported platforms
- [ ] Test case improvement


### Back port request
- [ ] 202205
- [ ] 202305
- [ ] 202311
- [ ] 202405
- [ ] 202411
- [ ] 202505
- [ ] 202511

### Approach
#### What is the motivation for this PR?

`pidof` scans all `/proc/<pid>/stat` entries. When a short-lived process
exits mid-scan, Linux removes its `/proc/<pid>/stat` before `pidof`
reads it. `pidof` logs the error **directly to syslog** via `syslog()`
syscall (not stderr):

```
ERR pidof[1549411]: can't read from 1549407/stat
```

This is a benign Linux race condition, but SONiC's `LogAnalyzer` catches
it as an unexpected `ERR`-level syslog message and marks the test run as
`errors=1` even when the test itself passes. `2>/dev/null` does not help
because the error goes to syslog, not stderr.

#### How did you do it?

Replace `pidof orchagent` with `pgrep -x orchagent` in:
- `operate_orchagent()` in `tests/bgp/bgp_helpers.py` (SIGSTOP and
SIGCONT commands)
- `is_orchagent_stopped()` in `tests/bgp/test_bgp_suppress_fib.py`

`pgrep` silently skips vanished processes and does not write to syslog.
The `-x` flag enforces exact name matching (equivalent to `pidof`
behaviour). Output format differs (newline vs space) but `.split()`
handles both.

#### How did you verify/test it?

Run `tests/bgp/test_bgp_suppress_fib.py` on a testbed — the base run
should no longer show `errors=1` from the `pidof` race condition.

#### Any platform specific information?

N/A

#### Supported testbed topology if it's a new test case?

N/A

### Documentation

N/A

commit.message:
Support for long paths in run command (#398)

`winapp run` fails on paths exceeding ~260 chars due to Windows MAX_PATH
limits. This adds `longPathAware` manifest support, a `LongPathHelper`
utility, and targeted workarounds for the two main failure points: Win32
build tools (MakePri/MakeAppx) and the WinRT `PackageManager` API.

## LongPathHelper

- `EnsureExtendedLengthPrefix` — adds `\\?\` (or `\\?\UNC\` for UNC
paths) to bypass MAX_PATH in Win32 file I/O
- `GetShortPath` — converts long paths to 8.3 form via
`GetShortPathName`; preserves trailing directory separators (critical
for sparse registration URIs); consistent `<= MaxPath` boundaries
throughout
- `GetShortPathOrThrow` — throws an actionable
`InvalidOperationException` when shortening fails (8.3 disabled, path
doesn't exist on disk)
- `ValidatePathLength` — fast-fails with a clear remediation message
when long paths are unsupported at the system level
- `GetShortPathRaw` — correctly restores `\\?\UNC\server\share\...` →
`\\server\share\...` (not the invalid `UNC\server\share\...`); handles
`DllNotFoundException` gracefully on non-Windows

## RunCommand

- Validates `inputFolder.FullName` early (before the status spinner) so
long-path errors surface before any file system work
- Also validates the resolved manifest path immediately after resolution

## PackageRegistrationService

- All three `PackageManager` call sites (`RegisterLooseLayoutAsync`,
`RegisterSparseAsync`, `InstallPackageAsync`) use `GetShortPathOrThrow`
instead of the silent `GetShortPath` fallback
- Sparse registration preserves the trailing separator on the external
location URI

## PriService

- Uses the extended-prefix `dumpPath` consistently for `File.Exists`,
`ReadAllTextAsync`, and `File.Delete` — not the unprefixed
`dumpOutputFile`

## Tests

Added `LongPathHelperTests` with 16 unit tests covering boundary cases
(`== MaxPath`, `> MaxPath`), UNC prefix handling, trailing separator
preservation, and `GetShortPathOrThrow` throw behavior.

commit.message:
[pfcwd] Disable memory utilization check for test_default_cfg_after_load_mg (#23732)

### Description of PR
Summary: Disable memory utilization check for `TestDefaultPfcConfig` to
avoid false-positive alarms when `config load_minigraph` restarts
containers.

ADO: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_workitems/edit/37433902

### Type of change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Testbed and Framework(new/improvement)
- [ ] New Test case
    - [ ] Skipped for non-supported platforms
- [ ] Test case improvement

### Back port request
- [ ] 202205
- [ ] 202305
- [ ] 202311
- [ ] 202405
- [ ] 202411
- [ ] 202505
- [ ] 202511

### Approach
#### What is the motivation for this PR?
`test_default_cfg_after_load_mg` calls
`config_reload(config_source='minigraph')`, which runs `config
load_minigraph -y` on the DUT and restarts swss and dependent containers
(swss, syncd, teamd, bgp, etc.).

The `memory_utilization` plugin captures a **before** snapshot and an
**after** snapshot for each test. When containers restart mid-test:
- **Before** snapshot: captured while containers are down → artificially
low RSS
- **After** snapshot: captured after containers fully recover → normal
RSS
- The delta easily exceeds the 20% threshold → **false-positive memory
alarm**

The `mg_cfg_setup` fixture teardown also calls
`config_reload(config_source='minigraph')` for state restoration,
compounding the issue.

#### How did you do it?
Added `@pytest.mark.disable_memory_utilization` to the
`TestDefaultPfcConfig` class in `tests/pfcwd/test_pfc_config.py`.

This is the same pattern already used in `tests/acl/test_acl.py` and
other tests that trigger container restarts.

#### How did you verify/test it?
Verified by analyzing a real failure log where `test_watchdog_reboot`
produced a false-positive memory alarm following a `test_warm_reboot`
failure that left containers down — the same root cause applies here.

#### Any platform specific information?
N/A — applies to all platforms where `config load_minigraph` restarts
containers.

#### Supported testbed topology if it's a new test case?
N/A

### Documentation
